name: Vinnytsia Oblast (Ukraine)

colors.symbolism: the historic azure of Podolia and the Bracław land
the upper and lower bars — Cossack and Podolian regimental heritage
the silver crescent and shield of the Bracław arms

description: A blue rectangular field with two narrow red horizontal stripes (one near the top, one near the bottom) and, in the centre, the historic arms of Podolia (a golden sun) and the Bracław land (a silver cross over a blue shield with a silver crescent).
history: Approved on 18 July 1997. The arms combine the heraldry of two historic territories that compose modern Vinnytsia Oblast: Podolia (the golden sun) and the Bracław land (eastern Podolia, with the silver cross and crescent).

features.symbolism: decorative red bar near the top edge
decorative red bar near the bottom edge

features.role: combined arms of Podolia (sun) and the Bracław land (cross-and-crescent shield)
